rationale: The query's transferable engine is an exact analytic self-consistent-equation solution for a phase-transition threshold on a network, where the threshold is set by moments of the degree distribution (diverging for heavy-tailed P(k)); the hyp_abstract re-expresses this as a resilience threshold from mean/second-moment of an exposure distribution. The candidate instead applies RMT eigenvalue analysis, empirical correlation-threshold network construction, and MF-DFA multifractality to real index time series — a purely empirical/statistical clustering and scaling analysis with no self-consistent cascade equation or degree-distribution-moment-derived critical point, so 'network' and 'threshold' are surface vocabulary rather than shared machinery. The object does genuinely differ from the query (spin/Ising dynamics on an abstract random graph) since the candidate studies real-world financial index correlation networks, so object_difference is TRUE even though the methods do not cohere.
